chapter_title.en: Chapter 187 – The (Satanic) insinuations and discussing with the self

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف على المشهور - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (11/266)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف على المشهور - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (11/266)
text: سَأَلْتُ أَبَا عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) عَنِ الْوَسْوَسَةِ وَ إِنْ كَثُرَتْ فَقَالَ لَا شَيْ‏ءَ فِيهَا تَقُولُ لَا إِلَهَ إِلَّا اللَّهُ .

en.hubeali: Al Husayn Bin Muhammad, from Moalla Bin Muhammad, from Al Washa, from Muhammad Bin Humran who said,

‘I asked Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> about the (Satanic) insinuations and if it was frequent. So he<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘There is nothing in it. You should be saying, ‘لَا إِلَهَ إِلَّا اللَّهُ’ ‘There is no god except for Allah<sup>azwj</sup>’.
en.sarwar: 1. Al-Husayn ibn Muhammad has narrated from Mu’alla ibn Muhammad from al- Washsha’ from Muhammad ibn Humran who has said the following: “Once I asked abu ‘Abd Allah (a.s.) ‘What if temptation (soul’s tale telling) becomes a great deal.’ The Imam said, ‘There is nothing in it. Say, ‘No one deserves to be worshipped except Allah.’”
